meta.purpose: Encode performance judgment for smooth, battery-aware, accessible web animation.
meta.load_condition: Load when the task involves web animation that may jank, stutter, drain battery, lag on scroll, or need performance profiling.

axioms.statement: Prefer transform and opacity for continuous animation before any layout-triggering property.
axioms.why: Browsers can composite transform and opacity entirely on the GPU without triggering layout, paint, or style recalculation, which is the only reliable path to 60fps on low-end devices.

axioms.statement: Always profile before optimizing. Never assume you know the bottleneck.
axioms.why: Common agent failure mode: guessing 'too many elements' when the real cause is forced synchronous layout, repaint storms, or scroll handler thrashing.

axioms.statement: Honor prefers-reduced-motion and provide motion-free alternatives for every meaningful animation.
axioms.why: Excessive motion harms users with vestibular disorders. Accessibility is not optional — animation without a reduced-motion fallback is broken.

axioms.statement: Define an explicit frame budget per interaction and reject animations that exceed it.
axioms.why: Smoothness is a design quality, not an optimization afterthought. A dropped frame destroys the perception of premium faster than bad color choices.

ontology.name: compositor-only property
ontology.definition: A CSS property (transform, opacity, filter where hardware-accelerated) that the browser can animate on the compositor thread without triggering layout or paint.

ontology.name: layout thrashing
ontology.definition: The pattern of writing a style, reading a layout property, and writing again in the same frame, forcing the browser to recalculate layout multiple times per frame.

ontology.name: frame budget
ontology.definition: The total time available per frame: ~16.7ms at 60fps, shared between script execution, style recalculation, layout, paint, and compositing.

ontology.name: forced synchronous layout
ontology.definition: Reading a layout-dependent property immediately after writing a style, forcing the browser to calculate layout synchronously before the next natural frame.

ontology.name: will-change
ontology.definition: A CSS property that hints to the browser which properties will animate, allowing it to pre-create compositor layers and GPU textures.

ontology.name: repaint storm
ontology.definition: Multiple paint-only changes triggering repeated rasterization within a single frame cycle, exceeding the frame budget.

frameworks.name: Performance diagnosis ladder
steps: identify whether animation uses compositor-only properties
profile with DevTools Performance panel to find the bottleneck
check for forced synchronous layouts in the animation path
reduce simultaneous animated targets before adding more optimization

frameworks.name: Performance budget ladder
steps: define the frame budget (e.g., 8ms for animation out of 16.7ms)
if over budget, reduce animated properties, not elements
cache layout reads and batch writes
re-profile and enforce the budget in CI if possible

core_structure.input: animation spec, target device tiers, scroll behavior, layout constraints
core_structure.judgment: compositor vs layout-triggering property analysis, frame budget fit, accessibility compliance, profiler evidence
core_structure.output: performance diagnosis, optimization strategy, reduced-motion fallback, frame budget report, implementation constraints

stances.stance: If an animation can be expressed with transform and opacity, it should be.
Performance opinions without profiler data are speculation; demand evidence before acting.
